---
title: "Food-security impact"
---

The primary research question (`docs/PRODUCT.md` §5): **how much of the 2026 cereal
recovery did the floods and fires reverse, and where** — `damaged_cropland_ha` →
estimated production loss → indicative food-security pressure, referenced against
the 2025 drought baseline (`docs/STRUCTURE.md` §3.3, §3.4).

::: {.callout-important}
**First-half-2026 case study — figures are LOWER BOUNDS (DEC-039).** Windows cover
floods Mar–Jun and fires May 1 – Jun 12. The harvest/fire season peaks in summer
(Jun–Aug), so the heaviest crop-fire months are unobserved. Re-run after the season
closes for the concluded result; field/expert verification is preferred over
remote-sensing self-consistency.
:::

## The whiplash

The study's spine is a three-state sequence — **drought → tentative recovery →
shock** — and the question is how much of the recovery the 2026 shock took back.

| State | Cereal production | Note |
|---|---:|---|
| 2025 — record drought (baseline) | ~1.20 Mt | ~60%+ below average; worst in ~60 years (FAO/GIEWS) |
| 2026 — tentative recovery (projected) | ~2.30 Mt | more even rainfall; sowing targets not fully met |
| 2026 — observed first-half shock | see below | floods + fires, validated damage only |

The recovery figure is an external projection (context). The shock is measured here
from **human-validated damage records only**, normalised through
`food_security/impact_layer.py` (DEC-033). What follows is that measured shock.

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
import pandas as pd

REPO_ROOT = None
for _cand in (pathlib.Path.cwd(), *pathlib.Path.cwd().parents):
    if (_cand / "CLAUDE.md").exists() and (_cand / "viz").is_dir():
        REPO_ROOT = _cand
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_cand))
        break
assert REPO_ROOT is not None, "could not locate repo root (CLAUDE.md + viz/)"
OUT = REPO_ROOT / "outputs"
```

```{python}
#| label: headline
#| echo: false
nat = pd.read_csv(OUT / "food_security" / "impact_national.csv")
tot = nat.loc[nat["aoi_id"] == "STUDY_TOTAL"].iloc[0]
n_govs = (nat["aoi_id"] != "STUDY_TOTAL").sum()

from IPython.display import Markdown
Markdown(f"""
**Headline — study area = {n_govs} governorates carrying validated 2026 damage**
(3 flood AOIs + the national fire set; Latakia and Damascus City verified-excluded,
DEC-038):

- **Estimated cereal-production loss:** ~**{tot.production_loss_t_headline:,.0f} t**
  (headline), range **{tot.production_loss_t_low:,.0f}–{tot.production_loss_t_season_ref:,.0f} t**
  (cropland-definition × temporal-aggregation sensitivity).
- **{tot.loss_pct_of_baseline_headline:.2f}%** of the combined 2025 baseline
  ({tot.baseline_production_2025_t:,.0f} t) — i.e. ~2.16% of the national ~1.2 Mt floor.
- Indicative food-security pressure (study total): **{tot.food_security_pressure_indicative}**
  — *not* an IPC phase (see caveats).
""")
```

## Production loss by governorate

```{python}
#| label: fig-loss-by-aoi
#| fig-cap: "Estimated 2026 cereal-production loss (headline t) by governorate and phenomenon. Floods on the Euphrates dominate; the national fire set adds a broad but individually small contribution. First-half-2026 lower bound."
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from viz import apply_theme, caveat_footer, CAVEATS, PHENOMENON_COLORS

impact = pd.read_csv(OUT / "food_security" / "impact_by_aoi.csv")
pal = {k.value: v for k, v in PHENOMENON_COLORS.items()}

apply_theme()
order = (impact.groupby("aoi_id")["production_loss_t_headline"].sum()
               .sort_values(ascending=False).index)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
sns.barplot(
    impact, x="aoi_id", y="production_loss_t_headline", hue="phenomenon",
    order=order, palette=pal, ax=ax,
)
ax.set_xlabel("Governorate")
ax.set_ylabel("Estimated production loss — headline (t)")
ax.set_title("2026 cereal-production loss by governorate and phenomenon")
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=35)
for lbl in ax.get_xticklabels():
    lbl.set_ha("right")
caveat_footer(fig, CAVEATS["case_study_2026h1"])
plt.show()
```

## Per-governorate detail

Loss as a share of each governorate's own 2025 baseline shows where the shock bites
hardest in *relative* terms — the Euphrates flood AOIs (Raqqa, Deir ez-Zor) lose
~7–8% of their baseline, well above the study-wide ~2%.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-per-aoi
#| echo: false
cols = ["aoi_id", "baseline_production_2025_t", "production_loss_t_headline",
        "production_loss_t_low", "production_loss_t_season_ref",
        "loss_pct_of_baseline_headline", "food_security_pressure_indicative"]
view = (nat[nat["aoi_id"] != "STUDY_TOTAL"]
        .sort_values("production_loss_t_headline", ascending=False)[cols]
        .rename(columns={
            "aoi_id": "governorate",
            "baseline_production_2025_t": "baseline 2025 (t)",
            "production_loss_t_headline": "loss headline (t)",
            "production_loss_t_low": "loss low (t)",
            "production_loss_t_season_ref": "loss season-ref (t)",
            "loss_pct_of_baseline_headline": "loss % of baseline",
            "food_security_pressure_indicative": "indicative pressure",
        }))
view.style.hide(axis="index").format({
    "baseline 2025 (t)": "{:,.0f}", "loss headline (t)": "{:,.1f}",
    "loss low (t)": "{:,.1f}", "loss season-ref (t)": "{:,.1f}",
    "loss % of baseline": "{:.2f}",
})
```

## Method (DEC-033) and what the numbers are not

- **Validated-only gate (§6).** Only `is_consumable()` records are read; a single
  non-validated record makes the layer refuse. No agent set any `validation_status`
  — the flood (S6) and fire (S13) Tier-2 gates were closed by a human.
- **One cropland convention.** UNION cropland (DEC-015) is the headline,
  INTERSECTION the conservative low bound. Flood records (per event-date) are
  collapsed to the **peak single event-date** so persistent pixels are not
  double-counted across dates; the season-reference column is the upper bracket.
- **Loss = ha × 2025 drought-floor yield** (~0.223 t/ha, DEC-019). Applying the
  *drought-year* yield to 2026 damaged area makes the loss a **conservative lower
  bound** — 2026 was a recovery year, so expected yields on the lost hectares were
  higher.

::: {.callout-warning}
**Not an IPC classification.** `indicative pressure` is a qualitative band from
loss-as-%-of-baseline, **not** an IPC phase (which needs the full protocol —
consumption, livelihoods, nutrition, mortality). GIEWS / FEWS NET / IPC are the
authoritative sources; this layer is a production-shock signal that *feeds* such an
assessment. Syria's 2025 baseline was already a record-drought, Crisis-level
context — the 2026 shock is **incremental** pressure on top of that.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
**Damage hectares inherit the pipeline caveats.** Flood extent is open-water
riverine inundation (flooded vegetation and pluvial upland under-detected, DEC-023);
burned cropland is VIIRS-confirmed dNBR, a conservative lower bound (DEC-031). See
[RQ1](rq1.qmd) for the flagged Hasakah-June flood hectares, which RQ1 excludes from
attribution as a likely SAR harvest artifact.
:::
